user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 16:03 EST
Subject: Re: Re: partner engagement Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with partner engagement. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 14:56 EST
Subject: URGENT: partner engagement Issue at Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery

Our team encountered some challenges with partner engagement. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 10:02 EST
Subject: Follow-up: partner engagement Review

Just checking in on the partner engagement proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on partner engagement from our last session.

[00:06:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:20:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - partner engagement

signals.detail: Signal detected for Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Calendar Pre-Pack Automation template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
